content:
# Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons

**Summary**: A class of organic compounds that are often present in crude oil and coal tar.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019]] analyzed PAHs to determine the specific types of hydrocarbons being sequestered by the mangrove.

- [[2019-alhaiqi|Alhaiqi, 2019]] studied three specific PAHs (Phenanthrene, Fluoranthene, and Pyrene) as indicators of contamination.

## Scientific Context

- [[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019]] used to identify specific types of hydrocarbon contamination.

- [[2019-alhaiqi|Alhaiqi, 2019]] studied three specific PAHs (Phenanthrene, Fluoranthene, and Pyrene) as indicators of contamination.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019]] quantified via chemical extraction and analysis.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Two and three ring PAHs were abundant in plant samples while high molecular PAHs were considerably higher in sediment. ([[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019, p. 115]])


- Phenanthrene, Fluoranthene, and Pyrene were selected because they are predominant in air, sediment, and water. ([[2019-alhaiqi|Alhaiqi, 2019, p. 42]])
